free_text: Measurement of (p,p) elastic differential cross
sections fo carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, aluminium and
silicon in  the 500-2500 keV range at 140 deg and
178 deg laboratory scattering angles
All samples were carefully investigated by 4He BS
experiments (EHe = 1600 and 2000 keV) to determine
the stoichiometry, layer thickness, homogeneity and
presence of impurities that could affect the results.
All samples are natural.
The annular detector, a Si surface barrier
detector, accepts particles scattered at 178 deg.
effective scattering angle and has a solid angle
of 18 msr and an energy resolution of 20 keV.
Another Si surface barrier detector is located at
140 deg. in IBM geometry, with a solid angle of
3.4 msr and an energy resolution of 15 keV.
Proof-read by Ana Rita L.Ramos Whal(2011-09-14)
Due to Signal area and areal density
SD
On. Revised data from Ana Rita L.Ramos Wahl
Aluminium and nitrogen where measured simultaneously
using a (1350+-70)x10**15/cm2 thick AlN film,
reactive sputter deposited over a vitreous graphite
substrate.
Over the AlN film, a gold layer (55 +-5)xl0**15/cm2
thick was evaporated.
4He BS experiments showed that the film was
stoichiometric (49 at.% N and 48 at.% Al).
Minor impurities of 0 (2.5 at. %) and Cu (0.5 at. %)
were detected.
Fig.4 of Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B190(2002)95
On. STATUS added
The carbon target consisted of a self-supporting,
(1200+-60)x10**15/cm2 thick C foil, prepared by
evaporation and coated with (270+-13)x10**15/cm2 gold,
also evaporated over its surface.
No impurities were detected in the sample.
Fig.1 of Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B190(2002)95
On. Uncertainty of Rutherford ratio added
Aluminium and nitrogen where measured simultaneously
using a (1350+-70)x10**15/cm2 thick AlN film,
reactive sputter deposited over a vitreous graphite
substrate.
Over the AlN film, a gold layer (55 +-5)xl0**15/cm2
thick was evaporated.
4He BS experiments showed that the film was
stoichiometric (49 at.% N and 48 at.% Al).
Minor impurities of 0 (2.5 at. %) and Cu (0.5 at. %)
were detected.
Fig.2 of Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B190(2002)95
On. STATUS added
Silicon and oxygen were also measured simultaneously
from a SiO2 film, (530+-50)x10**15/cm2 thick, argon
sputtered over a vitreous graphite substrate.
4He BS experiments gave a composition of 35 at.% Si
and 57 at.% O.
A slight argon and carbon contamination due to
specimen preparation was detected in the Si02 layer,
but it did not influence the measurements.
The Si02 layer was also gold coated
Ana Rita L.Ramos Wahl (2011-09-08):
The difference between uncertainty quoted for 178 deg
and 140 deg is due to the fact that the 178 deg data
had to be repeated at the time with less points and
smaller statistics due to beam time allotment
constraints.
Fig.3 of Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B190(2002)95
On. Uncertainty at 178 deg revised.
